hero.title: Pooja Tiwari
hero.tagline: Words that convert, stories that stick. I craft editorial content, SEO copy and brand narratives that move readers to act.

about.headline: Writing is the shortest distance between a brand and a believer.
about.bio: I'm Pooja Tiwari, a content writer and copywriter based between Nepal and Australia. For the past six years I've helped startups, agencies and established brands find their voice — translating complex ideas into clear, compelling stories that readers actually finish. From search-optimised articles to conversion-focused landing pages, my work is grounded in research, sharpened by craft, and measured by results.
about.philosophy: Good writing isn't decoration — it's architecture. Every sentence should earn its place by informing, persuading, or delighting. I write for the reader first and the algorithm second, because trust is what truly converts.

services.title: Content Strategy
services.description: Editorial calendars, audience research and tone-of-voice systems that turn scattered ideas into a coherent publishing engine.

services.title: SEO Writing
services.description: Research-led articles and landing pages engineered to rank — and to read like a human wrote them (because one did).

services.title: Brand Copywriting
services.description: Website, product and brand copy that sounds like you — only sharper, warmer and impossible to ignore.

services.title: Social Media Content
services.description: Scroll-stopping captions, threads and carousels that grow communities and start conversations.

services.title: Email Marketing
services.description: Welcome sequences and newsletters that nurture subscribers into loyal customers.

portfolio.title: The Quiet Power of a Well-Written Onboarding Email
portfolio.excerpt: Why the first message a user receives sets the tone for their entire relationship with your product.
portfolio.content: Onboarding is the most underrated page in your funnel. This long-form piece explores the psychology of first contact, breaking down three real-world sequences and the small copy changes that lifted activation by double digits. We look at subject lines, the art of the single CTA, and why clarity always beats cleverness in the inbox.

portfolio.title: 10 Local SEO Wins for Small Businesses in 2025
portfolio.excerpt: A practical, no-fluff checklist for capturing 'near me' searches without a big agency budget.
portfolio.content: Local search keeps changing, but the fundamentals are stubbornly consistent. This guide walks through Google Business Profile optimisation, review velocity, schema markup and the content gaps most local sites leave wide open. Each tactic includes a copy template you can deploy this week.

portfolio.title: Homepage Rewrite: From Features to Feelings
portfolio.excerpt: How a fintech startup tripled demo requests by rewriting three sections of its homepage.
portfolio.content: A breakdown of a real homepage overhaul: the before-and-after of a hero, a benefits-led feature grid, and a pricing page that finally made sense. We dissect the messaging hierarchy and the microcopy that removed friction at every step.

portfolio.title: API Documentation That Developers Actually Read
portfolio.excerpt: A field guide to writing developer docs that reduce support tickets and build trust.
portfolio.content: Technical writing is its own discipline. This piece covers information architecture for docs, the anatomy of a great quickstart, and how to write code samples that don't rot. Built from a year of maintaining documentation for an open-source SDK.

portfolio.title: A Letter to the Brand That Forgot Its Voice
portfolio.excerpt: A short, lyrical piece on why consistency of voice is the quietest form of brand trust.
portfolio.content: Sometimes the best way to make a point is to feel it. This creative essay personifies a brand losing its voice and finding it again — a favourite piece used in tone-of-voice workshops to show (not tell) teams what's at stake.

portfolio.title: The Ultimate Guide to B2B Thought Leadership
portfolio.excerpt: Stop publishing me-too listicles. Here's how to build genuine authority in a crowded niche.
portfolio.content: Thought leadership isn't a content format — it's a point of view. This comprehensive guide covers finding your angle, structuring long-form pieces, and the distribution habits that separate forgotten posts from industry references.

portfolio.title: Product Description Refresh for a Skincare Line
portfolio.excerpt: Sensory, compliant copy that turned browsers into buyers for a clean-beauty brand.
portfolio.content: A portfolio sample showing before/after product descriptions across a 24-SKU range, balancing evocative language with claim compliance. Includes the brief, the guardrails, and the results.

portfolio.title: Email Sequence: The 5-Touch Welcome
portfolio.excerpt: A annotated breakdown of a welcome sequence that nurtured 12k subscribers into customers.
portfolio.content: We open the hood on a five-email welcome flow: the hook, the story, the proof, the offer and the nudge. Each email is annotated with the strategic intent behind every line.

blog.title: Why I Write the conclusion before the introduction
blog.excerpt: A small habit that changed how I structure every piece of long-form content.
blog.content: Knowing where you're going makes the path obvious. In this journal entry I share the counter-intuitive outlining technique that cut my drafting time in half and made my arguments tighter. We look at real examples from client work and the science of cognitive load.

blog.title: The difference between good SEO and invisible SEO
blog.excerpt: Ranking is the easy part. Keeping the reader is the craft.
blog.content: Too many writers stop at the keyword. This piece argues that the real SEO skill is retention — writing the kind of content people finish, share and link to. I break down the on-page signals that actually matter in 2025.

blog.title: A copywriter's case against the exclamation point
blog.excerpt: Punctuation is persuasion. Here's when to put the excitement down.
blog.content: A short, opinionated take on restraint in copy. When everything shouts, nothing is heard — so I make the case for calm, confident writing and show three rewrites that landed harder without a single '!'.

testimonials.quote: Pooja rewrote our homepage and demo requests tripled within a month. She has a rare gift for finding the human truth inside a technical product.
testimonials.name: Aarav Sharma

testimonials.quote: The most reliable writer we've worked with. Her SEO articles consistently out-rank competitors twice our size, and they actually read beautifully.
testimonials.name: Elena Whitfield

testimonials.quote: Pooja shaped our entire tone of voice. Our brand finally sounds like a person, not a committee. Every team member references her style guide.
testimonials.name: Daniel Okafor

testimonials.quote: Working across two time zones was seamless. She delivered sharp, research-led copy on every deadline without sacrificing warmth.
testimonials.name: Mira Rai
